zh.All Saints Churchyard, Woodchurch, Ashfo: 英國英格蘭肯特奧爾平頓哥吉拉 沙智 亞德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Countess Bennetta Church於1298年出生在英國英格蘭肯特時，她的父親Albert Church28歲，母親Rhoda Wigglesworth22歲。她於1315年與Rev Peter Clerke在英國肯特阿福德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1325年在她的家鄉享年27歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特奧爾平頓哥吉拉 沙智 亞德。

zh-hans.All Saints Churchyard, Woodchurch, Ashfo: 英国英格兰肯特奧爾平頓哥吉拉 沙智 亞德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Countess Bennetta Church于1298年出生在英国英格兰肯特时，她的父亲Albert Church28岁，母亲Rhoda Wigglesworth22岁。她于1315年与Rev Peter Clerke在英国肯特阿福德结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1325年在她的家乡享年27岁，埋葬在英国英格兰肯特奧爾平頓哥吉拉 沙智 亞德。

nameMeanings.name: Church
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name mostly found in southern and midland England, for someone who lived near a church, or possibly an occupational name for someone who worked at a church, such as a verger or sexton, from Middle English chirche ‘church’. The word comes from Old English cyrice, ultimately from medieval Greek kyrikon, for earlier kyriakōn (dōma) ‘(house) of the Lord’, from kyrios ‘lord’. Compare Kirk .
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Kirch  .

variants.name: Kirk
meanings: Scottish, English, and Danish: from Middle English, Danish kirk ‘church’ (Old Norse kirkja), a topographic name for someone who lived near a church. This name has also been established in Ireland since the 16th century or earlier.

variants.name: Kern
meanings: German, Dutch, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German kerne ‘kernel, seed, pip’; Middle Dutch kern(e), keerne; German Kern or Yiddish kern ‘grain’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a farmer, or a nickname for a physically small person. As a Jewish surname, it is mainly artificial.
English (southeastern): metonymic occupational name for a maker or user of milk churns, from Middle English kern, kirn, kurn, a side-form of chern ‘churn’. Compare Church .
Slovenian: nickname for a stunted person, from an old spelling of krn ‘stunted’.

nameMeanings.name: Bennetta
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The normal medieval vernacular form of   Benedict  , borne by women as well as men in the past. Now sometimes used as an antiquarian revival, but more often a transferred use of the surname derived from the medieval given name.

variants.name: Bennet
meanings: The normal medieval vernacular form of   Benedict  , borne by women as well as men in the past. Now sometimes used as an antiquarian revival, but more often a transferred use of the surname derived from the medieval given name.

variants.name: Benett
meanings: The normal medieval vernacular form of   Benedict  , borne by women as well as men in the past. Now sometimes used as an antiquarian revival, but more often a transferred use of the surname derived from the medieval given name.

variants.name: Benet
meanings: From Church Latin benedictus ‘blessed’. This was the name of the saint ( c.480– c.550 ) who composed the Benedictine rule of Christian monastic life that is still followed in essence by all Western orders. He was born near Spoleto in Umbria, central Italy. After studying in Rome, he went to live as a hermit at Subiaco, and later organized groups of followers and imitators into monastic cells. In c.529 he moved to Monte Cassino, where he founded the great monastery that is still the centre of the Benedictine order. His rule is simple, restrained, and practical. See also Bennett .

en.lifeSummary: When Countess Bennetta Church was born in 1298, in Kent, England, United Kingdom, her father, Albert Church, was 28 and her mother, Rhoda Wigglesworth, was 22. She married Rev Peter Clerke in 1315, in Ashford, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1325, in her hometown, at the age of 27, and was buried in All Saints Churchyard, Orpington, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

fr.All Saints Churchyard, Woodchurch, Ashfo: All Saints Churchyard, Orpington, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Countess Bennetta Church est née en 1298, à Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Albert Church, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Rhoda Wigglesworth, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Rev Peter Clerke en 1315, à Ashford, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1325, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 27 ans, et a été enterrée à All Saints Churchyard, Orpington, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Lei sposato Rev Peter Clerke nel 1315, nel Ashford, Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1325, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 27 anni, e fu sepolta a All Saints Churchyard, Orpington, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Countess Bennetta Churchが1298年にイギリスイギリスケントで生まれた時，父Albert Churchは28歳，母Rhoda Wigglesworthは22歳であった。1315年にイギリスケントアシュフォードでRev Peter Clerkeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1325年に故郷で27歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスケントオルパントンアル サインツ チャーチヤルドにて埋葬された。

ko.All Saints Churchyard, Woodchurch, Ashfo: 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 르핀그턴 앨 세인츠 c후르c히아어드
ko.lifeSummary: Countess Bennetta Church(이)가 1298년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Albert Church(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Rhoda Wigglesworth(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1315년 영국 켄트 애스h퍼드에서 Rev Peter Clerke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1325년 그녀의 고향에서 27세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 르핀그턴 앨 세인츠 c후르c히아어드에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Rev Peter Clerke em 1315, em Ashford, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. ru.All Saints Churchyard, Woodchurch, Ashfo: алл сеинц чурчярд, орпингтон, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Countess Bennetta Church родилась в 1298 г. в местности кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Albert Church было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Rhoda Wigglesworth, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Rev Peter Clerke в 1315 г. в местности ашфорд, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1325 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 27 лет и была похоронена в местности алл сеинц чурчярд, орпингтон, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.
